description: A referendum held on March 17, 1992, for white South Africans to vote on whether to continue the negotiations to end apartheid. The 'Yes' vote resulted in a 68.7% majority for reform.

key_events.description: President F.W. de Klerk announces the referendum to solidify his mandate.
Conservative party campaigns heavily against the 'surrender of power'.
International community pressure mounts on white voters to choose reform.
Final results confirm an overwhelming mandate for change.

calibration_notes: Ideal for modeling a referendum used as a tool to bypass parliamentary opposition and secure a public mandate for systemic change.
